steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The task is to "provide an example of a poem from Morning Haiku." An example requires naming a specific poem and establishing it belongs to that book — general statements about themes, the author's motivations, or unnamed poems do not constitute an example of a poem.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Included in Sanchez's poetry book Morning Haiku (2010), "5 love haiku" is a poem written as a sequence of five haiku.: Names the specific poem "5 love haiku," explicitly places it in Morning Haiku, and gives a concrete structural detail about it. This is a genuine example of a poem from the book. ✓
  • Choice B — Sanchez chose to write in the form of the haiku because it helps "maintain memory and dignity.": Explains Sanchez's artistic motivation for using haiku — says nothing about any specific poem or that it appears in Morning Haiku. ✗
  • Choice C — The 2010 poetry book Morning Haiku "celebrates the gifts of life and mourns the deaths of revered African American figures.": Describes the overall theme of the book, not any specific poem within it. ✗
  • Choice D — The poem by Sanchez is written as a sequence of five haiku.: Uses "the poem" without naming it or tying it to Morning Haiku — too vague to serve as a concrete example. ✗
That is the correct answer.
